human_reference:
SUMMARY:
Added some comments with a quick look, I have not played with this yet, but overall approach looks good

POINTS:
- [installation-manager/src/main/java/org/wildfly/core/instmgr/InstMgrCertificateImportHandler.java:31] The Javadoc is similar on other handlers, better if we add specific documentation for each handler
- [installation-manager/src/main/java/org/wildfly/core/instmgr/InstMgrListUpdatesHandler.java:183] This should be handled via standard log files, otherwise, it won't use i18n. Reporting it as `OperationFailedExceptio` looks apropiated.
- [installation-manager/src/main/java/org/wildfly/core/instmgr/InstMgrPrepareRevertHandler.java:153] Will miss i18n
- [installation-manager/src/main/java/org/wildfly/core/instmgr/InstMgrPrepareUpdateHandler.java:195] Will miss i18n
- [installation-manager/src/main/java/org/wildfly/core/instmgr/InstMgrResourceDefinition.java:126] should be removed or add its own validator
- [pom.xml:243] 2.0.0.Beta1 is already out
- [installation-manager/src/main/java/org/wildfly/core/instmgr/cli/ListCertificatesCommand.java:31] All these new CLI commands need their documentation at https://github.com/wildfly/wildfly-core/blob/main/installation-manager/src/main/resources/org/wildfly/core/instmgr/cli/command_resources.properties
- [installation-manager/src/main/java/org/wildfly/core/instmgr/cli/AddCertificatesCommand.java:62] There is a constant for "cert-file"
- [installation-manager/src/main/java/org/wildfly/core/instmgr/cli/AddCertificatesCommand.java:79] String.format is redundant since there is no arguments for it
- [installation-manager/src/main/java/org/wildfly/core/instmgr/cli/AddCertificatesCommand.java:86] This should not be executed if the import is canceled
- [installation-manager/src/main/java/org/wildfly/core/instmgr/cli/AddCertificatesCommand.java:52] Both, buildParseOperation() and buildOperation() will send the same file as an attachment. A possible improvement would be to save the attachment somehow in the Installation Manager service so it can be referenced by the second operation without sending it again. It would be a minor improvement that will require a method to clean up the attachments. Consider it only if the attachment sizes are big
v8_review:
SUMMARY:
This PR adds certificate management operations for signature verification in the Installation Manager, including import, parse, remove, and list certificates functionality. The implementation has several issues including a missing parameter registration, incomplete error handling with a `// throw exception` comment left in code, unnecessary result assignments, dead code, and a brittle test mock.

POINTS:
- [installation-manager/src/main/java/org/wildfly/core/instmgr/InstMgrCertificateImportHandler.java:81] The else branch at line 81-82 contains only a comment `// throw exception` with no actual exception thrown. When neither `cert-file` nor `certificate-content` is provided, the operation will silently succeed without importing anything. This should throw an `OperationFailedException` with a message like "One of cert-file or certificate-content must be provided."

- [installation-manager/src/main/java/org/wildfly/core/instmgr/InstMgrCertificateImportHandler.java:46] The `CERT_CONTENT` attribute is defined (line 46) but is NOT registered as a parameter in the operation definition. The `DEFINITION` at line 51 calls `addParameter(CERT_FILE)` but never `addParameter(CERT_CONTENT)`. As a result, users cannot use the `certificate-content` option via the management API — it will be silently ignored or rejected as an unknown attribute.

- [installation-manager/src/main/java/org/wildfly/core/instmgr/InstMgrUnacceptedCertificateHandler.java:83-84] Inside the for loop, `context.getResult().set(info)` is called (line 84), but this is immediately overwritten by `result.set(mCertificates)` at line 93. This intermediate assignment is unnecessary and misleading — the final `result.set(mCertificates)` at line 93 is what actually matters. Remove the `context.getResult().set(info)` line inside the loop.

- [installation-manager/src/main/java/org/wildfly/core/instmgr/logging/InstMgrLogger.java:110] The new `mutuallyExclusiveOptions` message (id=27) reads "You cannot use the '%s' option with the '%s' option because they are mutually exclusive." This is used in InstMgrCertificateImportHandler when BOTH options are provided (correct usage), but the handler also needs a DIFFERENT error for when NEITHER is provided (the incomplete `// throw exception` at line 81). Consider adding a separate message like "One of '%s' or '%s' must be provided."

- [installation-manager/src/main/java/org/wildfly/core/instmgr/cli/AddCertificatesCommand.java:75] Uses `commandInvocation.print("Importing a trusted certificate")` but should use `println` to add a newline. Additionally, the response from `this.executeOp(ctx, this.host)` at line 76 is not checked for success — if the import fails, the user sees "Importing a trusted certificate" followed by a confusing exception.

- [installation-manager/src/main/java/org/wildfly/core/instmgr/cli/ListCertificatesCommand.java:76-88] The `getAllChannelNames` method is defined but never called anywhere in the class. This is dead code and should be removed.

- [testsuite/shared/src/main/java/org/wildfly/test/installationmanager/TestInstallationManager.java:336-348] The `parseCertificate` test mock uses `while (reader.ready())` which is unreliable — `BufferedReader.ready()` can return false even when data is available. This pattern can silently skip lines or fail intermittently. Use the standard `while ((line = reader.readLine()) != null)` pattern instead.

- [installation-manager/src/main/java/org/wildfly/core/instmgr/cli/UpdateCommand.java:91-92] When `importPendingCertificates` returns `false` (user declines to import certificates), the method returns `CommandResult.SUCCESS`. However, the update was cancelled and the user will be confused to see a success result. Consider returning `CommandResult.SUCCESS` but printing a clearer "Update cancelled" message, or consider returning a different result.
suspicion_review:
SUMMARY: This review addresses three confirmed issues in `InstMgrCertificateImportHandler.java` involving an unregistered operation parameter and an incomplete exception-handling path that causes silent success when no certificate input is supplied. Three additional hedged questions are included for author clarification regarding parameter registration, defensive CLI parsing, and test robustness.

POINTS:
- [installation-manager/src/main/java/org/wildfly/core/instmgr/InstMgrCertificateImportHandler.java:86] The `else` branch contains only a comment `// throw exception` but no actual exception is thrown. When neither `streamIndex` nor `certificateContent` is provided, the operation silently succeeds without importing any certificate.
- [installation-manager/src/main/java/org/wildfly/core/instmgr/InstMgrCertificateImportHandler.java:50] The `CERT_CONTENT` attribute is defined but never added to the operation definition via `.addParameter()`. Consequently, users cannot pass the `certificate-content` parameter, and the resolution logic in `execute()` becomes dead code.
- [installation-manager/src/main/java/org/wildfly/core/instmgr/InstMgrCertificateImportHandler.java:82] The `execute()` method contains an empty `else` block when neither `streamIndex` nor `certificateContent` is provided. Since both attributes are `setRequired(false)`, the method silently succeeds without importing a certificate, leaving the `// throw exception` comment unimplemented.

OPEN QUESTIONS:
- [installation-manager/src/main/java/org/wildfly/core/instmgr/InstMgrCertificateImportHandler.java:53] Could you clarify whether `CERT_CONTENT` was intentionally omitted from the operation definition, or if it might need to be registered via `.addParameter()` to allow users to pass the `certificate-content` parameter?
- [installation-manager/src/main/java/org/wildfly/core/instmgr/cli/AddCertificatesCommand.java:74] Would it be advisable to add a definition check before calling `asString()` on `CERT_KEY_ID` and related fields, in case the parse operation returns a result without them?
- [installation-manager/src/test/java/org/wildfly/core/instmgr/InstMgrResourceTestCase.java:1553] Might it be safer to use the standard `executeForResult()` helper or verify the `OUTCOME` before accessing `rsp.get(RESULT).asList()`, to prevent exceptions if the operation fails?
